frozen_components: LLM base weights (except embeddings and output layer adaption)
architecture.summary: A two-stage hybrid reasoning system that compresses chain-of-thought (CoT) traces using discrete latent tokens. Stage 1: A VQ-VAE (vector-quantized variational autoencoder) with a 50M-parameter encoder-decoder pair learns to map text token sequences into discrete latent codes organized in a fixed codebook. Stage 2: An LLM is fine-tuned on modified training data where the leftmost N CoT tokens are replaced with their latent abstractions (demarcated by special tokens), while later tokens remain as raw text. During training, N is randomly sampled per example to expose the model to varying degrees of latent-text mixing; at inference, the LLM generates latent tokens directly without invoking the VQ-VAE. The approach treats latent tokens as vocabulary extensions, requiring no external module at test time.
trained_components: LLM token embeddings for latent codes

biblio.title: Token Assorted: Mixing Latent and Text Tokens for Improved Language Model Reasoning

claims.statement: On Math benchmark with Llama-3.2-1B, latent approach achieves +4.2 percentage points over the second-best baseline (CoT).
On GSM8K benchmark with Llama-3.2-3B, latent approach achieves +4.1 percentage points over CoT.
On Fresh-Gaokao-Math-2023 benchmark with Llama-3.1-8B, latent approach achieves +13.3 percentage points over CoT.

claims.dataset: Math, GSM8K, Gaokao-Math-2023, DeepMind-Math, College-Math, OlympiaBench-Math, TheoremQA (aggregate)
claims.statement: Across all mathematical reasoning benchmarks tested (Math, GSM8K, Gaokao-Math-2023, DeepMind-Math, College-Math, OlympiaBench-Math, TheoremQA), latent approach achieves an average of 17% reduction in reasoning trace length compared to CoT while improving accuracy.
On Keys-Finding Maze synthetic benchmark with T5 trained from scratch, latent approach achieves 62.8% on 1-Feasible-10 metric, a +19.8 percentage point improvement over CoT (43%).
On ProsQA logical reasoning benchmark with GPT-2 trained from scratch, latent approach achieves 96.2% accuracy, a +18.7 percentage point improvement over CoT (77.5%).

claims.dataset: Math, GSM8K, and mathematical reasoning benchmarks
claims.statement: Randomized replacement strategy (varying the number of text tokens replaced per sample) consistently outperforms curriculum learning and all-replace strategies, enabling fast single-stage adaptation to unseen latent tokens without multi-epoch curriculum training.

Attention weight analysis shows that models trained with latent-text mixing focus more attention on numerically significant tokens and tokens representing mathematical operations (e.g., 'earns', 'cost'), compared to baseline CoT models, suggesting improved capture of essential reasoning information.
